title: Task status management and graph visualization for MCP workflow
purpose: Provides functions to update task statuses, retrieve task graphs, and broadcast updates via WebSocket for real-time collaboration.
module_context: Exports type definitions for task status management including interfaces for parameters and responses, and three main functions: updateTaskStatus, getTaskGraph, and updateTasksStatus.

methods.name: updateTaskStatus
methods.normalized_params: (params: UpdateTaskStatusParams, wsHandler?: { broadcast: (msg: any) => void }): Promise<TaskGraphResponse>

steps.content: Validate that all required parameters are present and the status is valid.
Load the current session state from storage.
Locate the specified task and update its status within the batches.
Recalculate the arrays of completed and pending tasks.
Update the status of the containing batch if all tasks are complete or in progress.
Save the updated state back to storage.
Generate a new Mermaid diagram representing the updated task graph.
Broadcast the updated task graph to connected WebSocket clients.
Return a response containing the diagram and updated state, or a minimal success response if requested.
Validate that required parameters are present.
Load the current session state from storage.
Generate a Mermaid diagram from the current task batches.
Return a response containing the diagram and current state.

methods.name: updateTasksStatus
methods.normalized_params: (params: UpdateTasksStatusParams, wsHandler?: { broadcast: (msg: any) => void }): Promise<TaskGraphResponse>

steps.content: Validate that all required parameters are present and updates are valid.
Load the current session state from storage.
Create a map of task IDs to new statuses for efficient lookup.
Update all specified tasks in a single pass through the batches.
Recalculate the arrays of completed and pending tasks.
Update the status of each batch based on its tasks.
Save the updated state back to storage.
Generate a new Mermaid diagram representing the updated task graph.
Broadcast the updated task graph to connected WebSocket clients.
Return a response containing the diagram and updated state, or a minimal success response with update details if requested.
